The top end of the Magic Millions National Weanling Sale delivered no shortage of fireworks, with fierce bidding around elite sire power, rare offerings and deep commercial pedigrees pushing four youngsters to $400,000 or more.

At the Magic Millions Gold Coast National Weanling Sale, TTR AusNZ uncovered a 'value buy' - an opportunity that a shrewd purchaser had snapped up. Trainer Simon Miller has had a decade of success buying weanlings at the Gold Coast and he hopes a daughter of Trapeze Artist can continue the trend.

Shuttle sire Starspangledbanner has a Classic winner with the dominant race-record breaking victory of Gstaad in Saturday’s G1 Irish 2000 Guineas at the Curragh. Jockey Ryan Moore won four races on the card including a pair of group races won by Wootton Bassett progeny.

The final Snitzel weanling to ever be offered at public auction will walk through the Gold Coast ring on Sunday as part of the 2026 Magic Millions Gold Coast National Weanling Sale. Managing Director Barry Bowditch highlights a catalogue he views as dense with quality and opportunity for pinhookers and end users alike.

Saturday at Doomben provided some thrilling black-type action, while Birdman capturing the G1 Doomben Cup was the highlight. Fireball Miss, Abounding, and Pinito added valuable stakes victories to their records and future breeding credentials.

Bjorn Baker’s exciting filly I Am Dirty announced herself as a serious filly with plenty of upside with a dominant Listed Bill Carter Stakes victory at Doomben, while promising juveniles by Farnan, Overshare and Almanzor also impressed across both sides of the Tasman.

Birdman looms as the horse most capable of testing Pride Of Jenni’s dominance in Saturday’s feature G1 Doomben Cup, with Chris Waller’s import primed to relish 2000 metres in a fascinating feature supported by quality races across Brisbane and Sydney.

Magic Millions is entering a fresh chapter, with James Hetherington elevated to Bloodstock Sales Manager and Dane Robinson moving into an expanded hybrid role spanning bloodstock and property, as the Gold Coast company continues to reshape.
